description_long: This kiosk features a symmetrical, classical design with a prominent central arched entryway supported by four sturdy columns. The structure is constructed from a material resembling weathered stone or concrete, giving it a robust and slightly aged appearance. On either side of the arch, there are rectangular counter-like surfaces, possibly for serving or display purposes, each supported by two columns. The roof is a semi-cylindrical barrel vault, adding to the architectural interest and providing shelter over the central area. The base is broad and slightly flared, ensuring stability and a sense of permanence. The overall look is reminiscent of neoclassical or Roman-inspired urban kiosks often found in parks or plazas.
description: A stone-like kiosk with a central arched entryway, barrel-vaulted roof, and column-supported counters on each side. The design is classical and robust, suitable for urban plazas or parks.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central arched entryway flanked by two rectangular counters and four supporting columns.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the semi-cylindrical barrel roof and the depth of the counters, with columns visible beneath.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually similar to the front, with the arch and columns mirrored, suggesting a throughway design.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the barrel roof and the structure's depth, mirroring view 1.
attribute_door_configuration: single open archway at center, front and rear
attribute_roof_design: barrel-vaulted roof, semi-cylindrical, made of the same stone-like material, no overhangs or decorative elements
attribute_architectural_style: neoclassical with Roman influences, featuring arches and columns
